$comment: SSOT contract for the script-evaluator /evaluate exchange. Consumed by services/script-evaluator vitest suite and services/workflow-orchestrator pytest suite. Version bumps require updating BOTH suites. callId derivation is FROZEN by this contract: baseHash = sha256(prompt + '\u0000' + canonicalJSON(semanticOpts)) hex; semanticOpts = {schema, model, effort, isolation, agentType, label} with undefined/null keys OMITTED and remaining keys sorted lexicographically at every nesting level (canonicalJSON); callId = baseHash[:40] + '_' + occurrence where occurrence is the per-baseHash encounter counter (0-based) in script issue order.

completedResultSemantics.done: agent() resolves with value (string, or parsed object when the call had opts.schema)
completedResultSemantics.skipped: agent() resolves with null (user skip via script.call.control)
completedResultSemantics.error: agent() resolves with null; errorCode may be error_max_structured_output_retries

workflowCompletedResultSemantics.$comment: kind='workflow' calls diverge from agent() on failure (Workflow-tool contract: workflow() THROWS so authors can try/catch).
workflowCompletedResultSemantics.done: workflow() resolves with value (the child run's returnValue)
workflowCompletedResultSemantics.error: workflow() THROWS Error(value.message ?? errorCode) into the script; errorCode is workflow_child_error (unresolvable ref / child script_error / child failure)
workflowCompletedResultSemantics.null: workflow() THROWS (legacy rows without a message throw 'workflow() child failed')

argsSemantics.$comment: request.args is the script's VERBATIM input — any JSON value (object, array, string, number, bool, null). KEY-ABSENCE is meaningful: when the request has no args key, the script's `args` global is undefined (Workflow-tool parity). The same applies to a kind='workflow' task's args field: omitted when the parent passed nothing.

responseRules: tasks MUST exclude every callId present in request.knownCallIds
newLogs MUST contain only log() lines with index >= request.seenLogCount; logCount is the total emitted so far
phases.current is the LAST phase() invoked before quiescence, or null if none

when budget.exhausted or budget.lifetimeExceeded, every UNRESOLVED agent()/workflow() call must observe a synchronously thrown BudgetExhaustedError/AgentLimitError; resolved (journaled) calls still resolve from completedResults
workflow() when request.nested=true must observe a thrown Error('workflow() nesting is one level only')
kind='workflow' tasks carry prompt='' and opts.schema=null; their spec is {callId, kind, workflowRef, args} — workflowRef/args appear in place of prompt/opts.label
a 4xx response (script_too_large, banned_api, invalid_meta) is NON-retryable; 5xx is retryable
